title: Error 'container ID cannot be mapped to a host ID' on podman pull as root with btrfs storage driver
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When pulling caddy:2.6.2-alpine as root on a system with btrfs storage driver, I get an error
`container ID 1001 cannot be mapped to a host ID`:

    $ sudo podman pull caddy:2.6.2-alpine
    (...snip...)
    Error: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob
    "sha256:f1747be7065842ba85f86aed65e75608dfd2f546ab9d8ecd4a8842c8f4634795": ApplyLayer stdout:
    stderr: container ID 1001 cannot be mapped to a host ID exit status 1

Notice I'm NOT running rootless here, so there should be no user namespace in play:

    $ sudo  podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map
    Error: please use unshare with rootless

In the sha256:f1747... blob, the file `usr/bin/caddy` is indeed owned by UID 1001:

    $ curl -sL -H "Authorization: Bearer $(curl -s 'https://auth.docker.io/token?scope=repository%3Alibrary%2Fcaddy%3Apull&service=registry.docker.io' | jq -r .access_token)" \
        https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/library/caddy/blobs/sha256:f1747be7065842ba85f86aed65e75608dfd2f546ab9d8ecd4a8842c8f4634795 \
        | tar tvz
    drwx------ 0/0               0 2022-10-14 03:43 root/
    drwx------ 0/0               0 2022-10-14 03:43 root/.step/
    -rwx------ 0/0               0 1970-01-01 01:00 root/.step/.wh..wh..opq
    drwxrwxrwt 0/0               0 2022-10-14 03:43 tmp/
    drwxr-xr-x 0/0               0 2022-08-09 10:47 usr/
    drwxr-xr-x 0/0               0 2022-10-14 03:43 usr/bin/
    -rwxr-xr-x 1001/121   40636416 2022-10-13 19:51 usr/bin/caddy


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Full debug log of image pull:

    $ sudo podman --log-level=debug pull docker://docker.io/library/caddy:2.6.2-alpine

**Describe the results you received:**

"Container ID 1001 cannot be mapped to a host ID"

**Describe the results you expected:**

No error

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Did not happen when system was configured to use overlayfs.

comments.body:
it works for me on Rawhide with btrfs.

I don't see why it should do any mapping.  Have you performed any change under `/etc/containers`?

Could you run podman under strace and attach here the log `sudo strace -f -v -s 1000 -Z podman pull ...` ?

After `rm -rf /var/lib/containers/{cache,storage}/*` the problem went away, I can now pull the image fine. This is the strace of when it was still in the broken state: [podman-pull-strace-221125.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/10095106/podman-pull-strace-221125.log). 

The configurations I have under `/etc/container` are:

- `storage.conf`: `storage.driver` = `"btrfs"`
- `registries.conf`: `unqualified-search-registries` = `["docker.io"]`

I'm still getting this on another host, even after nuking all of `/var/lib/containers/storage` and doing a reboot for good measure: [podman-pull-strace-221126-2.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/10096618/podman-pull-strace-221126-2.log).

I found something strange; I noticed podman is using UID 113000, which I recognize because (along with 22 other entries) I have this in `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid`:

    minecraft:113000:1000

This because I  create a container using the `--subuidname` and `--subgidname` options:

    podman container create --name minecraft --conmon-pidfile /run/podman/minecraft.pid --log-driver journald --log-opt tag={{.Name}} --net ... --ip ... --dns ... --dns-search ... --subuidname minecraft --subgidname minecraft --volume /srv/minecraft:/srv my.registry.io/minecraft:2209181011-1.19.2

When filtering the strace for %process and %file syscalls, I see this `execve`:

Hey @giuseppe, sorry for the delay, I did some more experimenting and managed to reproduce the problem with publicly available base images, and found a way as well to work around the problem. Here is the log you requested: [podman-pull-221130.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/10139633/podman-pull-221130.log).

As for the configs -- in `storage.conf`, I set `storage.driver` to `btrfs`; in `containers.conf` I have `unqualified-search-registries` set to `["docker.io"]`.

To reproduce the error with publicly available base images, do these steps as root:

1. Stop and destroy all containers
2. Create a clean slate: `rm -rf /var/lib/containers/{cache,storage}`
3. Create an alpine:3.16 container: `podman container create --uidmap 0:113000:1000 --gidmap 0:113000:1000 --name alpine docker://docker.io/library/alpine:3.16.3`
4. Create a caddy:2.6.2-alpine container: `podman container create --uidmap 0:122000:1000 --gidmap 0:122000:1000 --name caddy docker://docker.io/library/caddy:2.6.2-alpine`
5. The `/var/lib/containers/storage` tree now looks like this: [podman-storage-after-create-211202.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/10139770/podman-storage-after-create-211202.log)

And see that step 4 fails with `ERRO[0002] While applying layer: ApplyLayer stdout:  stderr: container ID 1001 cannot be mapped to a host ID exit status 1`.

By separately pulling the images first, the containers get created successfully:

1. Again stop and destroy all containers
2. Again create a clean slate: `rm -rf /var/lib/containers/{cache,storage}`
3. Pull both containers: `podman image pull docker://docker.io/library/{alpine:3.16.3,caddy:2.6.2-alpine}`
4. The `/var/lib/containers/storage` tree now looks like this: [podman-storage-after-pull-211202.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/10139772/podman-storage-after-pull-211202.log)
5. Create an alpine:3.16 container: `podman container create --uidmap 0:113000:1000 --gidmap 0:113000:1000 --name alpine docker://docker.io/library/alpine:3.16.3`
6. Create a caddy:2.6.2-alpine container: `podman container create --uidmap 0:122000:1000 --gidmap 0:122000:1000 --name caddy docker://docker.io/library/caddy:2.6.2-alpine`
7. The `/var/lib/containers/storage` tree now looks like this: [podman-storage-after-pull-then-create-211202.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/10139775/podman-storage-after-pull-then-create-211202.log)

Looking at what's different between the two scenarios, there's a difference in terms of ownership in the directories under `/var/lib/containers/storage/btrfs`, I attached the output of `tree -u -L 3` at different stages during the repros.

I think these errors are expected, you are using fewer IDs (1000) than what is needed for the image since it is using ID=1001.

At least with overlay, the different behavior is expected since we use idmapped mounts so it is fine to have IDs outside of the range as we don't really have to chown them.  We could add a check to behave in the same way but I don't see it as necessary since the kernel allows such a setup.

In the second case, does remapping even work?  Do files have the correct ownership?

so I looked into the code, when we chown an existing layer we on purpose map not existing IDs to the overflow ID, the same code path is used for BTRFS.

And it is me to blame for such change : https://github.com/containers/storage/commit/28c166fa18ee3ad9286cc5016d01a38c5baac10c

So I am closing since it works as expected
